title: Payment API Outage
summary: Checkout is returning 500s across all regions. Revenue is bleeding and an enterprise customer's engineering manager has joined the bridge demanding answers.

persona.name: Dana Reyes
persona.description: A sharp, time-pressured engineering manager at a top-tier customer. Wants concrete impact scope, a clear owner, and a status cadence. Escalates fast if she hears hedging or jargon without substance; calms when given honest specifics and a commitment.

stakes: Every minute of downtime costs the customer ~$4k in failed transactions and erodes a contract renewal due next quarter.
hidden_facts: Root cause is an expired TLS certificate on the payment processor gateway.
The outage began 12 minutes ago, right after a routine cert-rotation job silently failed.
A manual cert reissue restores service in ~3 minutes once identified.
Only card payments are affected; wallet payments still succeed.
objectives: Acknowledge the impact and reassure without over-promising
Gather and confirm the blast radius (regions, payment methods, error rate)
Identify the expired certificate as the likely root cause
Commit to a clear next update time and owner
opening_line: I've been staring at a wall of failed checkouts for ten minutes. What is actually going on, and when is it fixed?
